texto: Everything is a tool - a boot, a sled, a dog - and a hand, an arm, even a man! If it breaks down you throw it away and you march on! It's brutal, yes! And it's ugly. But anything else is sentiment and it will kill you.
explicacao: Citação de Ted Tally sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Everything is a tool - a boot, a sled, a dog - and a hand, an arm, even a man! If it breaks down …” articula tempo, decisions, vida em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como vida e sentiment.

texto: Playing the game means treating your dogs like gentlemen, and your gentlemen like dogs.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ted Tally: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Playing the game means treating your dogs like gentlemen, and your gentlemen like dogs.” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, antarctica, dogs pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dogs e gentlemen.

texto: Look out there. Can you feel them? Incredible to think - other human beings out there. You strain your eyes the whole day long, see nothing, hear nothing, still can't believe it somehow - but know it's true. Other warm bodies, hearts pumping blood. That ought to make us feel less lonely, or safer, it seems. Then why is it so shocking? Because - they don't belong here. The possibility of life in this place is more terrifying than the place itself. Can it be that we're really here?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ted Tally que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Look out there. Can you feel them? Incredible to think - other human beings out there. You strain…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, humans, vida, com foco em dia-chuvoso e vida.
